channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Iran’s Underground Missile Bases Withstand US and Israeli Airstrikes
item.description: Despite claims from former U.S. President Donald Trump that Iran's missile capabilities have been almost destroyed, recent intelligence reports show that Iran’s strike power remains formidable. Iran h...

item.title: Inside America’s Rapid Push for Domestic Uranium Enrichment
item.description: The article by physicist and environmental activist Lynda Williams examines how the United States is accelerating domestic uranium enrichment amid geopolitical tensions and a push for nuclear energy i...

item.title: Gallup Pakistan survey shows overwhelming public support for Pakistan’s role in US-Iran mediation
item.description: A new Gallup Pakistan survey reveals that an overwhelming majority of Pakistanis are highly supportive of their country’s mediation efforts in the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict. According to the fin...

item.title: Highlights and Recipes from Hot Thai Kitchen’s 10th Anniversary Edition
item.description: Hot Thai Kitchen: The 10th Anniversary Edition by Pailin Chongchitnant revisits the cookbook that introduced many Canadians to authentic Thai cuisine when it first debuted in 2016. Chongchitnant, a Va...

item.title: Lachie Kennedy breaks 10-second barrier with 9.96s run at Sydney nationals
item.description: In a landmark moment for Australian athletics, Queensland sprinter Lachlan 'Lachie' Kennedy became the first Australian to run the 100 metres in under 10 seconds on home soil. Competing at the nationa...

item.title: Local Players Key to Taifa Stars' AFCON 2027 Success
item.description: Tanzanians are dreaming big for their national football team, Taifa Stars, as they prepare for the AFCON 2027 finals hosted jointly by Tanzania, Kenya, and Uganda. Last time in Morocco, the team made ...

item.title: Timi Frank Accuses Tinubu’s Government of Targeting Opposition Ahead of Future Elections
item.description: Former APC deputy publicity secretary, Timi Frank, don come out sharply criticize President Bola Tinubu’s government, saying e dey use state power to weaken opposition parties before future elections ...

item.title: Macron's Shift: Kenyan President Ruto Takes Ramaphosa's Spot at G7 Summit
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa was unexpectedly disinvited from the upcoming G7 summit, following a dispute sparked by US President Donald Trump's objections to Ramaphosa's attendance. This m...

item.title: Elite Runners to Compete in Iconic Two Oceans Marathon Weekend in Cape Town
item.description: The Two Oceans Marathon returns to Cape Town this weekend, attracting thousands of runners for both the ultra-marathon and half marathon races. The ultra-marathon, set for Saturday, is 56 km and featu...

item.title: Governor Fubara Reaffirms Support for NLNG, Cites PPP Model as Key to Success

item.description: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) don announce say 2026 UTME candidates fit now print their exam slips. Dem don open di portal where candidates fit download di slip, which go show de...

item.title: New EU Biometric Border System: Key Information for Australian Travellers
item.description: From 10 April, Australians and other international visitors entering the Schengen zone will experience changes at European borders due to the EU's new Entry/Exit System (EES). The system replaces manu...

item.title: Boat Tragedy in Mathura: Six Dead as Rescue Operations Continue
item.description: A tragic accident occurred on the Yamuna River in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, when a boat carrying devotees capsized. The incident took place near Kesi Ghat in the afternoon, with one of the two boats sin...

item.title: Escaped South Korean zoo wolf prompts public interest and safety measures
item.description: A two-year-old male wolf named Neukgu escaped from the O-World zoo in Daejeon, South Korea, by burrowing under a fence, and has remained at large for three days. Weighing approximately 35 kilograms, t...

item.title: Doctors’ strike negotiations remain deadlocked as NHS faces mounting costs and uncertainty
item.description: Polly Toynbee’s column in The Guardian examines the stalemate between resident doctors in England and the Labour government over pay negotiations. After a temporary truce following Labour’s 22.3% pay ...
